milestone.status: all eight direct submitter callers have literal/call evidence captured
milestone.meaning: The shared submitter is used by auth, play-launch, session lifecycle, telemetry/event, and generic JSON request paths. This makes it a transport boundary shared by several client subsystems rather than a login-only helper.

call_evidence: Calls header append helper 0x1400E3130 before the shared submitter.
Calls string-copy helpers before passing R8 to 0x14062BCE0.
No endpoint literal is embedded in this function, so the endpoint appears caller-provided.

argument_shape.rcx: request object through RBX loaded from [R15]

call_evidence: Formats or appends flattened form parameters before the submitter call.

argument_shape.rcx: request object copied from caller RCX/RBP
argument_shape.r8: flattened form parameter string selected from [RSP+0x20]

call_evidence: Builds Authorization and JSON/Accept headers before composing the endpoint.
Uses the shared auth/request global [0x140E58A78] as the request object.

argument_shape.r8: body/payload string at [RBP-0x30], selected by capacity >= 0x10 small-string storage logic

call_evidence: Builds bearer authorization and mixed form/json headers before submit.
Uses the shared auth/request global [0x140E58A78] through RBX.
Constructs a JSON social-auth request body with Steam provider and account state fields.
Submits to v1/social/auth through the shared submitter.

argument_shape.rcx: request object through RBX loaded from [R15]

call_evidence: Formats the session/open endpoint and optional userHash query before submit.
Uses an empty JSON object body.

argument_shape.rcx: request object through RBX loaded from [RDI]

call_evidence: Formats the session/close endpoint and duration/userHash query variants before submit.
Uses an empty JSON object body.

argument_shape.rcx: request object through RBX loaded from [RSI]

call_evidence: Selects one of dismissal/click/impression event paths.
Formats a four-part event endpoint with userHash before submit.
Passes the constant empty JSON object literal 0x140B9AC18 as R8.

argument_shape.r8: constant empty JSON object literal at 0x140B9AC18

opennxt_947_implications: OpenNXT compatibility work should keep these endpoint families grouped under one async request-object model instead of treating auth, session, social, play-launch, and telemetry as unrelated transports.

The 947 client uses normal HTTP-shaped request concepts here: endpoint path, query string, content-type/accept headers, authorization header, and JSON/form bodies are all built before the submitter boundary.
Session open/close and telemetry calls use an empty JSON object body in the mapped paths, while social auth and play launch build richer JSON/form bodies.
The generic JSON submit wrapper at 0x1400ED4CC still needs upstream caller tracing because its endpoint is caller-provided rather than embedded as a literal.
next_steps: Trace upstream callers of 0x1400ED4CC to name its caller-provided endpoint family.
Trace the game-session body producer before 0x14029B047 to name the JSON fields passed in R8.
Use endpoint names from this artifact to align OpenNXT auth/session/social/play-launch handler expectations with the client's request lifecycle.
